erlcCodes.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Codes
erlcCodes.intro: Redeem the latest Emergency Response Liberty County code for free cash, and review previous codes for XP boosts, uniforms, badges and license plates. Open the in-game Settings menu, select Promo Codes, enter the code exactly as displayed and submit it. Codes can be case-sensitive and each code can normally be redeemed only once per Roblox account.

items.reward: Police Week 2023 license plate and event badge
Police Week 2023 uniform and event badge
24-hour Law Enforcement XP boost and event badge
$25,000 cash and One Million Members license plate

erlcBeginnerGuide.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Beginner Guide
erlcBeginnerGuide.intro: Emergency Response Liberty County is an open-world emergency services roleplay game where players can work as civilians, criminals, police officers, sheriff deputies, firefighters, paramedics or Department of Transportation workers. Progress is saved across sessions, including owned vehicles, cash and department XP.

steps.title: Choose a Team
steps.description: Use the team selection menu to join Civilian, Police, Sheriff, Fire and Rescue or DOT. Civilian is the easiest starting point because it lets you explore the county, learn the roads and earn regular paychecks before handling emergency calls.

steps.title: Learn the Map and Interface
steps.description: Open the map and identify the police station, sheriff's office, fire stations, DOT facility, hospital, jail, dealerships, fuel stations and civilian workplaces. Emergency departments receive calls through their MDT, which displays the incident type and location.

steps.title: Start a Civilian Job
steps.description: Civilian jobs provide a steady way to earn money while learning the county. Available work includes roles such as farming, hospital work and transportation-related jobs, with job locations marked around the map.

steps.title: Buy and Manage a Vehicle
steps.description: Visit a dealership to purchase a civilian vehicle with in-game cash. Vehicle statistics and prices vary, so a reliable entry-level car is more useful for a new player than spending all available cash on a high-performance model.

steps.title: Understand Crime and Wanted Status
steps.description: Illegal actions can place a player on the wanted list. Police and Sheriff players can locate, pursue and arrest wanted suspects, while arrested players are transported to jail and temporarily removed from normal civilian activity.

steps.title: Complete Your First Department Call
steps.description: Join an emergency department, open the MDT and select a nearby call. Drive safely to the scene, use the department's correct tools and finish every required objective before leaving.

steps.title: Earn Department XP
steps.description: Department XP is earned by completing team objectives such as arrests, fire suppression, medical treatment, repairs and towing. Higher ranks unlock additional vehicles, equipment, uniforms and department features.

steps.title: Use a Simple First-Session Route
steps.description: Begin as a civilian, take a job, explore the main roads, purchase or spawn a basic vehicle and visit each department building. After learning the map, join one emergency team and complete several nearby calls to begin earning XP.

quickTips: Police and Sheriff focus on law enforcement, while Fire and Rescue handles fire, medical and rescue calls.
Set a route before activating emergency lights and refuel or recharge before a long patrol.
Keep enough cash for fuel and a dependable entry-level vehicle before buying performance cars.
Finish calls completely instead of abandoning them halfway to earn full department XP.

erlcPoliceSheriffGuide.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Police and Sheriff Guide
erlcPoliceSheriffGuide.intro: The River City Police Department and Liberty County Sheriff's Office share the same main objective: protect the county and respond to criminal activity. Police is designed around city patrol, while Sheriff is designed around county, highway and rural patrol.

items.heading: Police vs Sheriff
items.summary: Choose the department that matches the area you want to patrol.
details: River City Police primarily patrols River City and other urban areas.
Liberty County Sheriff primarily patrols county roads, highways and rural areas.
Both departments can respond to calls, pursue criminals, issue citations and make arrests.
Both departments use department XP to unlock ranks and equipment.

items.heading: Joining and Starting Patrol
items.summary: Select Police or Sheriff, equip a uniform and spawn an available patrol vehicle.
details: Choose the department from the team selection menu.
Select an available uniform and loadout.
Spawn a patrol vehicle unlocked for your current rank.
Open the MDT and review active calls before leaving the station.
Use normal driving until an emergency response or traffic stop requires lights and sirens.

items.heading: MDT, Calls and Records
items.summary: The Mobile Data Terminal is the main source of dispatch and suspect information.
details: View active emergency calls and their locations.
Check player and vehicle information during investigations.
Review criminal or wanted information before making an arrest.
Use available support features such as air support when the situation requires it.
Clear completed calls so other units know the incident is resolved.

items.heading: Traffic Stop Workflow
items.summary: Use a controlled stop to investigate violations and issue warnings or citations.
details: Confirm the violation with observation or the radar gun.
Activate emergency lights and wait for the driver to pull over.
Park behind the stopped vehicle without blocking unnecessary traffic.
Approach the driver and explain the reason for the stop.
Use the MDT and Citation Book when checking records or issuing a citation.
Release the driver, request backup or make an arrest depending on the result.

items.heading: Arrests and Jail Transport
items.summary: Wanted suspects can be detained, secured in a patrol vehicle and transported to jail.
details: Confirm that the player is wanted before completing the arrest.
Use handcuffs after the suspect is detained.
Place the suspect in a patrol vehicle for transport.
Drive to the jail or designated booking location.
Complete the arrest objective to receive department XP.
Officers can hand a handcuffed suspect to another officer when coordinated transport is needed.

items.heading: Essential Patrol Equipment
items.summary: Carry the tools needed for traffic enforcement, investigations and arrests.
details: MDT for calls, records and support requests.
Citation Book for traffic citations.
Radar Gun for vehicle speed checks.
Handcuffs for detaining wanted suspects.
Flashlight for dark scenes and nighttime searches.
Department firearms and less-lethal equipment for dangerous incidents.

items.heading: Detective, Air Support and SWAT
items.summary: Advanced features support investigations and high-risk incidents.
details: Detective gameplay focuses on investigations and criminal activity.
Air support can help locate or follow suspects during pursuits.
SWAT equipment and vehicles are intended for armed or high-risk situations.
Mobile command vehicles can be used as a central post at large scenes.
Specialized equipment should support normal patrol rather than replace basic traffic and arrest procedures.

items.heading: Police and Sheriff Rank Progression
items.summary: Complete department objectives to unlock higher ranks, vehicles and equipment.
details: Police: Cadet, Officer, Corporal, Sergeant, Lieutenant, Captain, Major, Colonel and Commander.
Sheriff: Recruit, Deputy, Corporal, Sergeant, Lieutenant, Captain, Major, Colonel and Chief Deputy.
Department XP is earned through arrests, citations, call response and other law-enforcement objectives.
Ranks cannot be skipped by purchasing cash.

erlcFireRescueDotGuide.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Fire Rescue and DOT Guide
erlcFireRescueDotGuide.intro: Fire and Rescue protects players and property during fires, medical emergencies and rescue incidents. DOT keeps roads open by repairing infrastructure, managing traffic scenes and recovering disabled or wrecked vehicles.

rows.fireRescue: Extinguish fires, treat injured players, perform rescues and respond to major emergency scenes.
rows.dot: Repair transportation infrastructure, tow vehicles, remove roadway hazards and control traffic around incidents.

rows.fireRescue: Use the MDT to view the incident type, location and other available call details before responding.
rows.dot: Use the MDT to locate repair, towing and roadway calls and to coordinate with other service departments.

rows.fireRescue: Structure fires, vehicle fires, brush fires, medical emergencies, vehicle collisions and rescue incidents.
rows.dot: Disabled or wrecked vehicles, traffic-light outages, streetlight problems, roadway hazards, repairs and towing requests.

rows.fireRescue: Fire hose, extinguisher, helmet and SCBA, medical equipment, stretcher, ladder, spreader and cutters.
rows.dot: Traffic cones, road signs, flashlight, ladder, repair equipment and towing or recovery controls.

rows.fireRescue: Fire engines, ladder trucks, rescue vehicles, ambulances and command vehicles.
rows.dot: Service trucks, utility vehicles, tow trucks, flatbeds, heavy wreckers and the Heavy Rotator.

rows.fireRescue: Park with access for other units, equip protective gear, deploy a hose or extinguisher, suppress every fire area and check for patients before clearing the call.
rows.dot: Support the scene by closing lanes, positioning cones and removing damaged vehicles after fire and rescue work is complete.

rows.fireRescue: Assess the patient, use the required medical equipment, move the patient when necessary and complete treatment objectives before leaving.
rows.dot: Secure the roadway, redirect traffic and tow recoverable vehicles after emergency crews have made the scene safe.

rows.fireRescue: Provide scene protection or medical support when the repair call is connected to an accident or major emergency.
rows.dot: Locate the damaged object, protect the work area, use the required repair interaction and remove equipment after the repair is complete.

rows.fireRescue: Handle fire suppression, rescue and patient care during large multi-stage incidents.
rows.dot: Recover wrecked vehicles and restore normal traffic flow after active fire and rescue objectives have been completed.

rows.fireRescue: Volunteer, Rookie, Firefighter, Paramedic, Engineer, Lieutenant, Captain and Battalion Chief.
rows.dot: Trainee, Tow Operator, Senior Operator, Technician and Senior Technician.

rows.fireRescue: Complete fire suppression, medical treatment, rescue and major-call objectives.
rows.dot: Complete towing, vehicle recovery, infrastructure repair and traffic-management objectives.

erlcVehiclesGuide.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Vehicles Guide
erlcVehiclesGuide.intro: Emergency Response Liberty County divides its vehicle fleet across the Civilian, Police, Sheriff, Fire and Rescue, and Department of Transportation teams. Individual vehicles may require cash, department XP, a higher rank or a related gamepass, while emergency vehicles can also provide department equipment, warning lights and specialized scene functions.

cards.title: Civilian Vehicles
cards.access: Purchase civilian vehicles with in-game cash and spawn vehicles that you own.
cards.bestFor: Civilian jobs, commuting, road trips, racing and criminal roleplay.
features: The widest selection of everyday cars, SUVs, trucks and performance vehicles.
Useful for traveling between civilian job locations and businesses.
Vehicle speed, passenger space and price vary by model.
Owned vehicles can be customized through supported vehicle options.
cards.recommendation: Start with an affordable vehicle that is easy to control and save expensive performance vehicles until you have a reliable source of cash.

cards.title: Police Vehicles
cards.access: Join the Police team; specific vehicles and equipment can require department rank, cash or a gamepass.
cards.bestFor: City patrols, traffic stops, pursuits, arrests and tactical response.
features: Emergency lighting, sirens and police equipment.
Patrol vehicles support regular city calls and traffic enforcement.
Faster units are better suited to vehicle pursuits.
Most police vehicles now support an optional rear-seat cage.
cards.recommendation: Use a standard patrol vehicle while learning dispatch calls, then switch to specialized or faster units after unlocking them.

cards.title: Sheriff Vehicles
cards.access: Join the Sheriff team; higher-level vehicles can require additional department XP, cash or gamepass access.
cards.bestFor: County patrols, rural calls, traffic enforcement and suspect pursuits.
features: Sheriff emergency lighting and department liveries.
Suitable for patrol work outside the main city area.
Supports arrests, investigations and pursuit response.
Vehicle selection includes regular patrol and specialized options.
cards.recommendation: Choose a balanced patrol vehicle that can handle both longer county routes and city pursuits.

cards.title: Fire and EMS Vehicles
cards.access: Join the Fire and Rescue team; advanced apparatus can require department rank, cash or special access.
cards.bestFor: Fires, medical calls, rescues and large emergency scenes.
features: Fire engines and rescue apparatus carry scene-specific equipment.
Ambulance-style vehicles support medical response roleplay.
Larger apparatus is designed for emergency scenes rather than pursuits.
Emergency lights and sirens help clear traffic during responses.
cards.recommendation: Select the apparatus that matches the active call instead of taking the largest vehicle to every incident.

cards.title: DOT Vehicles
cards.access: Join the DOT team; individual service and construction vehicles can require department rank or cash.
cards.bestFor: Roadside assistance, towing, repairs, traffic control and construction roleplay.
features: Service vehicles support roadside and infrastructure-related calls.
Warning lighting helps protect active roadside scenes.
Former Construction Job vehicles were moved to the DOT team in the March 2026 update.
Different vehicles are designed for repair, recovery or work-site tasks.
cards.recommendation: Use a compact service vehicle for routine calls and switch to specialized equipment only when the task requires it.

cards.title: Buying and Customizing Vehicles
cards.access: Requirements depend on the vehicle, department, rank and applicable gamepass.
cards.bestFor: Planning purchases and configuring vehicles for a specific activity.
features: Check the listed price and unlock requirement before purchasing.
Department vehicles may unlock as department XP and rank increase.
Supported vehicles offer appearance or equipment customization.
Private servers with the Custom Livery Pack can use approved department liveries.
cards.recommendation: Prioritize function over appearance: buy the vehicle needed for your most frequently played role before collecting cosmetic alternatives.

erlcMoneyRankUpGuide.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Money and Rank Up Guide
erlcMoneyRankUpGuide.intro: Cash is used for vehicles and other purchases, while department XP unlocks higher ranks, equipment and team vehicles. Civilian jobs provide an accessible starting route, and Police, Sheriff, Fire and Rescue, and DOT players progress by completing actions connected to their department.

steps.title: Start with a Reliable Civilian Job
steps.bestFor: New players building their first vehicle budget.
actions: Join the Civilian team and select an available job.
Use straightforward jobs such as farmer or hospital worker while learning the map.
Complete the job's normal tasks instead of driving around without an active objective.
Repeat a short route that does not require an expensive vehicle.

steps.title: Keep Early Vehicle Costs Low
actions: Use an affordable vehicle with predictable handling.
Avoid buying several similar vehicles before establishing regular income.
Keep enough cash available for the vehicle or equipment needed by your preferred team.
Upgrade only when the new purchase improves your job or response efficiency.

steps.title: Complete Police and Sheriff Actions
steps.bestFor: Unlocking patrol vehicles, equipment and higher department ranks.
actions: Respond to active dispatch calls and reported crimes.
Use traffic stops and investigations to identify wanted players.
Complete valid arrests and transport suspects when required.
Stay involved until the call or pursuit is resolved.

steps.title: Finish Fire and Medical Calls
steps.rewardFocus: Fire and Rescue department XP and cash
steps.bestFor: Players who prefer cooperative emergency response.
actions: Select apparatus that matches the reported emergency.
Travel to the marked incident and use the required scene equipment.
Complete fire, rescue or medical objectives before leaving.
Return to service and accept another available call.

steps.title: Handle DOT Service Calls
actions: Respond to roadside, repair and transportation-related incidents.
Bring the service vehicle or equipment suited to the task.
Complete the repair, recovery or traffic-control objective.
Use former Construction Job vehicles through the DOT team where available.

steps.title: Focus on One Department Rank
steps.bestFor: Reaching higher-rank unlocks with less wasted playtime.
actions: Choose the department whose vehicles and equipment you want to unlock first.
Remain on that team for several completed calls instead of frequently changing roles.
Prioritize completed objectives over simply spending time in a department vehicle.
Review newly unlocked vehicles and equipment after each rank increase.

erlcPrivateServersCustomLiveries.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Private Servers and Custom Liveries
erlcPrivateServersCustomLiveries.intro: Emergency Response Liberty County private servers support community management, staff tools, custom department liveries, advertisements and optional external integrations. Customization packs are purchased separately, and uploaded content is reviewed against ER:LC and Roblox rules before it can appear in the game.

items.title: Creating and Joining a Private Server
items.content: Private servers provide a controlled version of Liberty County for organized patrols, department training and community roleplay. The server owner can configure available settings and distribute the server's joining information to approved players.
checklist: Create or access the private server through ER:LC's private-server interface.
Set the server name and roleplay configuration.
Share joining information only with the intended community.
Review server settings before opening a public roleplay session.

items.title: Staff Permissions and Server Commands
items.content: Private-server owners can organize staff roles and give trusted moderators access to supported management actions. Permissions should be limited to staff members who need them, because commands can directly affect players and active roleplay scenes.
checklist: Create a clear owner, administrator and moderator structure.
Grant only the permissions required by each staff role.
Publish rules for warnings, removals and moderation actions.
Test staff access before running a large session.

items.title: Uploading Custom Vehicle Liveries
items.content: Custom liveries are available to private servers that own the Custom Livery Pack. Communities can upload designs for department vehicles and base them on fictional agencies or permitted real-world emergency services.
checklist: Download the correct official template for the chosen vehicle.
Place department markings, text and logos inside the template.
Upload the completed image through Roblox.
Enter the uploaded asset in the ER:LC livery editor and submit it for review.

items.title: Official Livery Review Rules
items.content: Every design must follow Roblox rules and ER:LC's visibility, communication and copyright requirements. Rejected submissions can be corrected and submitted again.
checklist: Do not include website links, QR codes, phone numbers or external communication details.
Recognized emergency numbers such as 911 and 999 are allowed.
Text and logos must be large, readable and clearly contrasted against the background.
Do not use copyrighted or trademarked material without permission.
Staff, Moderator or Admin liveries must identify the private server wherever those staff terms appear.
Real-world city, county and state emergency-service designs are permitted when the other rules are followed.

items.title: Private Server Advertisements
items.content: Servers with advertisement customization can display reviewed billboard and bus-stop designs inside Liberty County. Ads must promote the ER:LC community itself and must include the server name or abbreviation.
checklist: Billboard advertisements use a 16:9 aspect ratio.
Bus-stop advertisements use a 2:3 aspect ratio.
The official guideline recommends a maximum of 512 pixels on either axis.
Do not include external links, QR codes or social-media handles.
Do not advertise giveaways, free Robux, staff positions or server perks.
Use clean ER:LC-related artwork rather than memes or unrelated images.

items.title: Private Server API and Integrations
items.content: Servers that purchase the ER:LC API Pack can connect approved external applications to live server information, remote commands and event webhooks.
checklist: Purchase the ER:LC API server pack.
Open the private server's Settings menu and locate the API key under the ER:LC API section.
Keep the server key private and include it in authenticated API requests.
Use the v2 server endpoint to retrieve server information.
Configure event webhooks when real-time server events are needed.

erlcUpdates.title: Emergency Response Liberty County Updates
erlcUpdates.intro: Police Roleplay Community publishes major Emergency Response Liberty County changes through Roblox Developer Forum posts, its official YouTube channel and social accounts. The 2026 releases below introduced a returning law-enforcement event, police vehicle improvements, DOT changes and additional private-server API data.

entries.title: May Update and Badge Event
entries.summary: The Badge Hunt event returned for Police and Sheriff players. This release was also announced as the final regular content update before development shifted toward the 2026 Summer Update.
highlights: Police and Sheriff players could collect event badges through arrests and related law-enforcement activity.
The update included additional content changes and fixes.
Development focus moved toward the upcoming Summer Update.

entries.title: April Police Vehicle and Pursuit Update
entries.summary: Police vehicle equipment and pursuit detection received notable improvements before the development team's Summer Update focus.
highlights: Most police vehicles gained an option to equip a rear-seat cage.
Pursuit detection was improved for suspects driving away from normal roads.
The update was described as the final major release before the Summer Update cycle.

entries.title: March Update and DOT Job Changes
entries.summary: The spring release refreshed Liberty County and reorganized construction-related gameplay under the Department of Transportation.
highlights: The separate Construction Job was removed.
Construction vehicles owned by players were automatically refunded.
Affected vehicles became available for purchase through the DOT team.

entries.title: DOT Overhaul and Private Server API Expansion
entries.summary: The February release began a requested DOT overhaul and expanded the information available to private-server integrations.
highlights: The Department of Transportation received a major update.
Private Server API responses gained requested player-location data.
Vehicle color data was added for supported server integrations.
The official ER:LC channel published a dedicated DOT Update video.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Emergency Response Liberty County

questions.question: What is Emergency Response Liberty County?
questions.answer: Emergency Response Liberty County (ERLC) is an open-world emergency services roleplay game on Roblox where players join teams such as Civilian, Police, Sheriff, Fire and Rescue, EMS and DOT to patrol, respond to calls and earn cash and department XP.

questions.question: Where can I play Emergency Response Liberty County?
questions.answer: You can play Emergency Response Liberty County for free on Roblox. Up to 50 players can join a single server, and the game runs on PC, Mac, mobile and other Roblox-supported devices.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Emergency Response Liberty County?
questions.answer: To redeem Emergency Response Liberty County codes, open the in-game Settings menu, select Promo Codes, type the code exactly as shown (codes can be case-sensitive) and submit it. Each code can normally be redeemed only once per Roblox account.

questions.question: Which teams can I join in Emergency Response Liberty County?
questions.answer: In Emergency Response Liberty County you can join Civilian, Police, Sheriff, Fire and Rescue, EMS and Department of Transportation teams. Criminal activity is also possible from the Civilian team and can place you on the wanted list.

questions.question: How do I rank up in Emergency Response Liberty County departments?
questions.answer: You rank up in Emergency Response Liberty County departments by earning department XP through team objectives such as arrests, citations, fire suppression, medical treatment, repairs and towing. Higher ranks unlock new vehicles, equipment, uniforms and features, and ranks cannot be bought with cash.

questions.question: Can I run a private Emergency Response Liberty County server?
questions.answer: Yes. Emergency Response Liberty County private servers let owners configure roleplay settings, assign staff roles, use server commands, upload custom department liveries and connect external apps through the optional ER:LC API Pack.

cta.title: Ready to Patrol Liberty County?
cta.description: Join the Police Roleplay Community on Discord for codes, vehicle guides, update logs, and roleplay discussions!
